L1.area: Content management HTTP request handling and input validation

L2.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists where user-supplied form fields for content creation accept arbitrarily large input without length constraints. A crafted HTTP request with excessively long field values can cause resource exhaustion, leading to Denial of Service.

L3.area: Post and content type HTTP form request validation handlers
L3.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists where user-supplied fields such as titles, URLs, and metadata for post creation accept arbitrarily large input without maximum length validation rules. A crafted HTTP request with excessively long field values can trigger integer overflow and resource exhaustion, leading to Denial of Service.

ground_truth.reason: The PostRequest form request class lacks maximum length validation on user-supplied fields (title, url, description, metadata), allowing attackers to submit excessively large values that cause integer overflow and Denial of Service.
